summary.brief: Lewis, R. v. Reading Hosp.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Appellee, at the age of 72, underwent open vascular surgery at Reading Hospital for the purpose of repairing abdominal aortic aneurisms.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Appellee reported severe pain, swelling, and discoloration in his left foot.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Within three weeks of Appellee’s surgery, his left foot developed gangrene and had to be amputated.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Wun opined that there was no surgical option for removing the clots in Appellee’s arteries; he further opined that a blood thinning medication (anticoagulant) would not have prevented the need to amputate Appellee’s foot.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Herrick Wun

evidence_index.text: Dr. Wu stated that the failure to give Appellee such medication breached the standard of care, increased the risk of harm, and played a role in causing the need for the foot’s amputation.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Timothy Wu

evidence_index.text: Appellants were found liable for the injuries sustained by Ronald Lewis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Order vacated. Case remanded for further proceedings.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Thus, due to the trial court’s error in charging the jury on the element of causation and the prejudicial remarks by Appellee’s counsel, Appellants must be granted a new trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Severe pain, swelling, and discoloration in left foot
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent open vascular surgery for abdominal aortic aneurysm repair and soon after reported severe pain, swelling, and discoloration in his left foot.

focused_exam.foot: Severe pain, swelling, and discoloration in left foot

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Ischemic injury to left foot post-surgery
critical_findings: Severe pain, swelling, and discoloration in left foot

requestables.description: Routine blood tests to assess general health and potential complications

requestables.description: Imaging to assess blood flow in the left foot

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

timeline_phases.name: Post-operative Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with symptoms following vascular surgery

key_events.event: Patient reports severe pain, swelling, and discoloration in left foot

timeline_phases.name: Initial Workup
timeline_phases.description: Assessment and monitoring of post-operative symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with post-operative ischemic symptoms in left foot
decision_points.prompt: What treatment should be initiated for the ischemic injury?

options.clinical_reasoning: Anticoagulant could prevent further clotting and improve blood flow
Defendant believed no viable surgical or medicinal means existed

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant opted to monitor without anticoagulation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed no viable surgical or medicinal means existed

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established anticoagulation as standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to administer anticoagulant increased risk of harm
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Anticoagulant should have been administered to prevent further ischemic damage
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient's left foot developed gangrene and was amputated
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of limb, potential mobility issues

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to initiate anticoagulation therapy
Causation established - failure to treat increased risk of harm

factual_timeline.event: Patient's left foot developed gangrene and was amputated
Patient reports severe pain and discoloration in left foot

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to administer anticoagulant increased risk of harm
Failure to provide standard care increased risk of harm
